intitule: School Life and Authorities: Vocabulary Matching

enonce.texte: Match the school-related vocabulary terms (1-8) with their correct definitions (A-H).

questions.texte: Principal
Staff
Subjects
Classroom
Bursar
Discipline Master
Secretary
School

enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences with the appropriate word from the box: principal, staff, subjects, classroom, bursar, discipline master, secretary, school.

questions.texte: The ________ is the head of the school and is responsible for all its activities.
All the teachers and other employees of the school are called the ________.
Mathematics, English, and History are examples of ________ taught at school.
Students learn in a ________ where the teacher gives lessons.
The ________ is responsible for managing the school's finances and fees.
The ________ is in charge of maintaining order and discipline among students.
The ________ handles the school's administrative tasks and correspondence.
A ________ is an institution where students go to receive education.

intitule: Applying School Vocabulary in Real-Life Contexts

enonce.texte: Read the following scenarios and use the appropriate school vocabulary to answer the questions.

questions.texte: A student needs to pay school fees. Which school authority should he/she visit?
A student has been misbehaving in class. Who is responsible for handling this situation?
A parent wants to know about their child's academic progress. Which school authority should they contact?
questions.reponse: They should contact the principal or the class teacher.

questions.texte: A student needs a letter of recommendation from the school. Who should they approach?
questions.reponse: They should approach the principal or the secretary.

intitule: Understanding the Roles of School Authorities in Cameroon

enonce.texte:
Read the text below and answer the questions that follow using your knowledge of school vocabulary.

"In many secondary schools in Cameroon, the principal is the highest authority. He/she is assisted by a team of staff members who ensure the smooth running of the school. The bursar manages the financial affairs, while the discipline master enforces rules and regulations. Teachers are responsible for teaching different subjects in their classrooms. The secretary handles administrative tasks. All these authorities work together to create a conducive learning environment."

questions.texte: According to the text, who is the highest authority in a Cameroonian secondary school?
questions.reponse: The principal is the highest authority.

questions.texte: What is the role of the bursar in the school?
questions.reponse: The bursar manages the financial affairs of the school.

questions.texte: Why do the school authorities work together?
questions.reponse: They work together to create a conducive learning environment.

enonce.texte: Read each of the following descriptions and identify the school authority being described. Choose from: principal, discipline master, bursar, secretary, teacher, student, staff.

questions.texte: This person leads the school and represents it in official matters.
This person manages the school's money, collects fees, and pays salaries.
This person ensures students obey the school rules and maintain discipline.
This person gives lessons and helps students learn different subjects.
This person handles the school's administrative work, like filing documents and writing letters.

intitule: Writing About School Life in Cameroon

enonce.texte: Imagine you are writing a paragraph about your school in Cameroon. Describe your school and its authorities using at least five of the following vocabulary words: school, principal, staff, subjects, classroom, bursar, discipline master, secretary.

questions.texte: Write your paragraph here.
questions.reponse: My school is a large secondary school in Yaoundé. The principal is a very dedicated and hardworking person who ensures everything runs smoothly. The staff are very cooperative and work together to create a good learning environment. We study many subjects like English, Mathematics, and History in our spacious classrooms. The bursar is responsible for collecting school fees, and the discipline master maintains order among the students. I am proud to be a student at this school.
